pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states a technician is salvaging hardware.
setup_task.sh creates assets named 'Dell Precision 7920 - Render Station' and 'CAD Station' with 'ASSET-ENG-*' tags, confirming the industry context.
setup_task.sh inserts this asset as the dead workstation ('DP7920-LIQ-FAIL') and export_result.sh queries its final status.
setup_task.sh inserts this asset as the active workstation, and export_result.sh checks for components checked out to this tag.
Task description strictly requires this name, and export_result.sh validates it by querying name LIKE '%NVIDIA RTX A6000%'.
Task description strictly requires this name, and export_result.sh validates it by querying name LIKE '%128GB DDR4%'.
Task description strictly requires this order number, and export_result.sh queries and exports it.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on synthetically generated data injected into the Snipe-IT database via setup scripts. The dead asset tag 'ASSET-ENG-99' (the failed workstation) and target asset tag 'ASSET-ENG-42' (the active workstation) are hardcoded and explicitly verified via the setup and export scripts. The task requires creating components named 'NVIDIA RTX A6000 48GB' and '128GB DDR4 ECC RAM Kit' with the order number 'SALVAGE-ENG-99', which are also verified against the task description and SQL queries in export_result.sh.
